To the person I randomly met on the school overpass. It started as a simple ripple in a quiet pond. I remember the day we met, a moment that felt entirely random, yet now feels like the only point on the map worth finding. I wasn’t looking for a story to write, but there you were, a sudden burst of color in a monochrome world. We were strangers, bound only by the coincidence of being in the same place at the same time, unaware that a single "hello" would eventually rewrite my entire internal geography. Then came the talking, where we built an entire world out of words. I remember every random topic we chased down rabbit holes and every long, heavy rant you trusted me to hold. I listened to the fractures in your voice when you spoke of the shadows in your past, those horrifying echoes that still make you hesitant to step into the light of something new. I wanted to be the silence that followed your storms. I truly thought I was just happy to know you, but I didn’t realize I was slowly weaving my life into the spaces between your sentences. Somewhere between the late-night echoes and the shared lessons, the truth hit me with a weight I couldn't ignore. I wasn’t just a spectator in your life anymore; I had fallen for you, deeply and without a safety net. I know I’m not the trendy type of guy who fits the polished mold of what people expect, but I would risk everything for the chance to be near you. I would stand by you as you confront those old ghosts, not because you are broken, but because no one should have to walk through the dark alone. We are in this together, even if that "together" is still just a hope I carry in my heart. It has been years, all the way back to the halls of Senior High, since I have felt a pull this tectonic. Now that I am in my third year, I realize how rare this is. And yet, I see the hesitation in your eyes. I see you pulling back into the mist, afraid that your past defines your future. I’m so sorry if you feel like you’ve made a mistake by letting me in, but you haven’t. I understand the weight you carry, and I respect the walls you’ve built to survive. If my presence is too loud, or if the love I’m offering feels like a burden you aren't ready to shoulder, then I will become a ghost. I will retreat into the background and fade into the static of the everyday, watching from afar as you grow and bloom into the person you are meant to be. I am content to be a memory if it means you can find your peace. I am just happy to see you exist, even if it is from the other side of a bridge I am no longer allowed to cross. — @Dhruv on Churv

I didn’t see that coming at all. Out of everything she could’ve said, that was the one thing I wasn’t ready for. Especially not after she already knew the story behind my surname—the real story, not just the easy version. She knows where it came from, why it exists, all the weight and history tied to it. That’s not something I open up about lightly. So when she said she wanted it... like she actually wanted my surname attached to hers... I just froze for a second. I was genuinely shocked. Not in a bad way—just caught off guard. Because in my head, once someone knows that kind of history, I don’t expect them to step closer to it. If anything, I expect a bit of distance. But she did the exact opposite. And the way she said it wasn’t serious or heavy. She was smiling. Happy. Like it was something she was excited about, something she *liked*. That part hit me even more. I don’t think she realizes how much that meant to me in that moment. There was something about seeing her be so light and sure about it that made me feel this weird kind of happiness I can’t really explain properly. Like... out of all the things tied to my name, she saw something worth choosing. Not something to avoid, not something to question—something she wanted to carry too. And honestly, that stayed with me. I’m still kind of shocked when I think about it. But at the same time, I can’t deny how much I enjoyed that moment—just hearing her say it so naturally, like it already belonged to her. It’s a simple thing on the surface, but it didn’t feel simple to me. It felt real. — @ on Churv

My journey Ever since I was in elementary school, I’ve felt the heavy weight of depression and anxiety pressing down on me. Every now and then, it feels like everything comes crashing in – as if there’s something huge and burdensome resting on my shoulders that I can never shake off. Even as a young adult, I finally understood why I’d always felt different from the other kids when I was younger. While they were making friends and chatting happily, I’d often find myself sitting off to the side, just watching them. I never really managed to make many friends – probably because I’m such an emotional person. Sometimes I can’t explain why I’d suddenly cry, or why my heart would race for no clear reason. All I ever wanted was someone who could understand me fully, but I always struggled to find that kind of connection. When I got to high school, I thought things might change – that maybe here I’d find people I could truly bond with. But nothing shifted; the sadness and unease only grew heavier as time went on. I’d worry if I was doing something wrong, or if others were thinking badly of me. So many thoughts would fill my head that didn’t need to, yet I couldn’t stop myself from overthinking everything. Then came college – I was hopeful I’d finally find friends who’d accept and understand me. I did make a few friends, and they’re really nice to get along with. We share laughs during breaks, eat meals together in the cafeteria, and help each other out when assignments pile up. They’re warm and friendly, always making sure I don’t feel left out when we’re in a group. It feels good to have people around me who treat me with kindness, who ask how my day was, and who include me in their plans – even small things like grabbing coffee or studying together. Now, I’ve realized I need to take a step back and pause for a while – not because of my friends, but to focus on myself and what I truly need. I want to figure out who I am at my core, understand my emotions better, and learn how to face the depression and anxiety that have been with me since I was a child. I want to become stronger, more resilient, and truly ready to nurture the friendships I have now, as well as any new ones that come my way. I know it won’t be easy, and there will surely be more challenges ahead, but I’m ready to face all of that for my own sake – and for the people who’ve shown me kindness too. I need to prioritize my mental and emotional well-being, because I know that’s what matters most right now. Maybe as I take time to build myself up and understand who I am, I’ll be able to be an even better friend to those who’ve been good to me, and find even deeper connections with people who truly get me – people who will stand by me, understand me fully, and walk with me every step of the way. Kayanon lang gihapon self bisan [REDACTED_NAME] and you want to rest, stay strong. you can still find someone who deserves all that you have to give. - Unknown

PLS READ (long post ahead) I want to believe there is still hope for this country, but it becomes harder when I see how quickly a person’s death is turned into mockery, and how labels like “NPA” are thrown without proof, as if words carry no weight and lives carry no value. As iskos and iskas, we are expected to be the hope of the nation. Yet hope cannot exist where critical thinking is absent. We should not be easily swayed by noise, nor should we be quick to judge what we do not fully understand. TRUTH demands patience, and justice demands restraint. We must also REMEMBER that the freedoms we enjoy today were not born from silence. They were carved by those who dared to speak, to question, and to resist indifference. To forget this is to MISUNDERSTAND the very foundation of our privilege. This is no longer a matter of colors in politics, nor of sides to choose in a divided crowd. It is a matter of conscience. We must rise above labels and learn to see one another as human first, before anything else. Please lang, dili nata mga bata. We are at a stage where we should be critical about everything we see and hear, especially when it involves our fellow youth. — @marginoftruth on Churv

It wasn’t our first encounter, but I felt something new, something unfamiliar. There’s nothing special about it, yet I was drawn to him like a moth to a light. I noticed him—every small detail: how he walks, talks, runs, yells, and smiles. I felt his gentle soul, and that made me fall deeper. The feeling continues to grow, even when all I ever did was try to prevent it. I didn’t want to feel this way, yet the heart never listened. I cried, I prayed, hoping that it would fade eventually, but it didn’t. Now, I’m left aching in the depths of this feeling. Why do we need to feel this way? I hate [REDACTED_NAME]. ✨ There’s this girl in our engineering class. I won’t say her name. She’s small, quiet most of the time—but when she speaks, everyone listens. Not because she’s loud, but because she’s always right. You know the type... the one who makes solving problems look effortless while the rest of us are still trying to understand the question. She’s smart. Like, really smart.But she’s also cute in a simple, natural way.And yeah... a lot of people admire her. Actually, “a lot” is an understatement. She’s one of our college representative, so she’s always busy—competitions, events, responsibilities. People approach her all the time. Confident guys, top students, even student leaders. The kind of people who don’t hesitate. And then there’s me. Just another engineering student trying to survive deadlines, quizzes, and sleepless nights. At first, I just respected her. Anyone would. But somewhere along the way, I started noticing the little things—how she focuses, how she explains, how she smiles when something finally makes sense. Now I find myself looking for her in class without even realizing it. But here’s the thing—I already know where I stand. She’s not just “hard to get.” It’s more like... she’s in a different world. A world where people are already lined up, trying to get her attention, trying to be part of her life. So I chose to stay quiet. Not because I’m scared (okay, maybe a little), but because I don’t want to be just another distraction. She has bigger things to focus on. Bigger goals. Sometimes we talk—just small things about school. And honestly, those moments are already enough for me. I don’t need her to notice me the same way. I guess this is one of those situations where you just admire someone from a distance. You support them quietly. You cheer for them without them knowing. And maybe that’s okay. Not everything needs to be confessed.Not every feeling needs an answer. Sometimes, you just carry it with you...and keep moving forward. - Tall Dark and Ugly — @TDU_2 on Churv

We are writing to formally express our concern regarding the recent decision to shift the proposed project from a dedicated study or tambayan area to a pickleball court. We understand that the officers have made this choice with the intention of benefiting the community, but we believe this change may not align with the actual needs and interests of the majority. The original proposal for a study or tambayan area was widely supported because it addressed a common need for a space where members could gather, relax, study, and socialize comfortably. This space would have been accessible and useful to everyone, regardless of their hobbies or physical abilities. In contrast, the shift to a pickleball court raises significant concerns. From our observation and interactions within the community, pickleball is not a widely played or known sport among us. Most members are not players of this game, and there is a strong likelihood that the court would be underutilized. We worry that allocating resources and space to a facility that caters to a small subset of the community would be an inefficient use of our funds and available area, leaving the majority without a space that meets their daily needs. We kindly request that the officers reconsider this decision and revisit the original proposal for a study or tambayan area. We believe this would better serve the collective interest and ensure that the project benefits as many members as possible. — @guesshoe on Churv

As a military officer who came up through ROTC myself, I hear you — and I understand where your frustration is coming from. When you’re sick or dealing with something serious and feel unheard, it stings. That should never be the experience we give our cadets. But let me share the other side, cadet to cadet, because I’ve been where you are now. Why I still encourage first-year students to consider ROTC for NSTP 1. Discipline is tough, but it’s not heartless by design ROTC runs on structure and accountability because the military has to. Lives depend on following orders and showing up. Sometimes that rigidity is misapplied by junior officers or cadet leaders who are still learning themselves. That’s a leadership failure — not proof the entire program lacks empathy. In my unit now: “Check on your people first”. A cadet who’s sick isn’t a weak link; they’re a responsibility. If that didn’t happen for you, then we — the officers — failed you, and I’m sorry for that. 2. What [REDACTED_NAME] gave me that classrooms couldn’t - Crisis leadership: You learn to make decisions when you’re tired, wet, and hungry. No NSTP component teaches that like field training does. - Service beyond self: We don’t just march. We’re first responders during typhoons, earthquakes, and relief ops. I’ve seen cadets younger than you carry sacks of rice through floods in [CARAGA] because people needed them. - Scholarships & career paths: ROTC opens doors to AFP commissionship, advanced ROTC scholarships, and even civilian jobs that value leadership and discipline. - Brotherhood/sisterhood: The bonds you form when you’ve gone through hardship together? Those are for life. My best friends today are my former ROTC classmates. 3. On “excuse letters being ignored” That’s not the standard. Under the IRR of RA and AFP regulations, valid medical certificates and excuse letters MUST be considered. If they weren’t, that’s on the implementing cadet officers of that unit. Don’t let one bad implementation define the whole program for you. Escalate it. Every ROTC unit has a Commandant and a school NSTP coordinator. Use that chain of command — it exists to protect cadets. *My challenge to incoming first-years Don’t just choose CWTS or LTS because you heard [REDACTED_NAME] is “hassle.” Choose ROTC if: - You want to test your limits and grow a backbone for real-world pressure. - You want to serve the country in a direct, physical way when disasters hit. - You’re willing to hold leaders accountable when empathy is lacking — because good cadets become better officers who fix these problems. ROTC isn’t perfect. We’re working on it. The AFP is actively training cadet officers on mental health, empathy, and cadet welfare precisely because of feedback like yours. Be part of the batch that makes it better instead of avoiding it. To the student who posted this: I won’t invalidate your experience. You deserved to be heard. But I also won’t let one story scare away students who could find purpose here. If you’re still enrolled, DM me. I’ll help make sure your concern reaches the right Personnel. To the incoming freshmen: “[REDACTED_NAME]”, but don’t choose based on fear. Choose based on who you want to become 4 years from now. If you want to lead, serve, and be ready when the country calls — ROTC will forge you. “Leadership is not about being in charge. It’s about taking care of those in your charge.” That’s what we teach. That’s what I lived. And that’s what I’m asking you to help us uphold. — 2LT [REDACTED_NAME] (OS) PA Proud ROTC Product, Class of Former Corps Commander, [CARAGA]-ROTCU Serving the Filipino People, Securing the Land. Always. Padayon, future cadets. The country needs you more than you know. — @Mdzm_Lee on Churv

For several administrations of the LSG, this initiative has been consistently mentioned, yet to this day, there is still no concrete space established for it. Students continue to make do with inadequate spaces, often left studying wherever they can, without a proper environment conducive to learning. And just in case the response to this concern is, “bayad muna college fee or reklamo oo, [REDACTED_NAME],” it’s important to clarify that many of us are responsible enough to pay our fees on time, because commitments were made. These expectations didn’t come out of nowhere; they were based on what was promised to us as students. It is difficult to overlook the contrast between the rapid momentum behind recreational projects and the prolonged delay in delivering a basic academic necessity. A dedicated study hub is not a luxury; it is a fundamental support system for students. The effort toward campus development is appreciated, but it is equally important to ensure that commitments made in the past are fulfilled. To be honest, online and blended learning have their advantages, and I totally get why many of us at [CARAGA] find them helpful, especially with the heat index past 40°C and the cost of commuting and boarding piling up. But personally, I think face-to-face classes are essential for us to fully engage, collaborate, and actually learn from one another rather than just teaching ourselves at home. Online classes can sometimes feel like just dumping materials on us. Let’s face it, not everyone can learn effectively on their own, and even in face-to-face classes, teachers often don’t have enough time to discuss everything properly, so they end up cramming lessons anyway. On top of that, not everyone has stable Wi-Fi or devices that can handle online classes, and when materials are posted, they’re sometimes delayed or too long and only uploaded close to the deadline. Teachers also don’t always see messages or queries from students, and even when they do, responses can take a while, which is understandable since they’re also handling a lot, but it still affects how we keep up with lessons. Being in class gives us the chance to ask questions right away, clarify concepts, and really understand the material instead of struggling alone. We’ve already experienced this during the pandemic, and a lot of us know how hard it was to stay motivated, keep up with lessons, and actually learn in a fully online setup. Some managed, but many also struggled silently, and I think that says a lot about how effective it really is for everyone. Maybe the solution isn’t to abandon online tools completely, but to combine them thoughtfully, keeping recorded lectures or LMS access as backup while making the main learning happen in person. This is just my opinion, and I hope it’s understood in the spirit of discussion, not as me dismissing anyone’s struggles with heat or transportation. I’d really appreciate it if we could share thoughts respectfully and focus on finding the best way to learn effectively together. — @marginoftruth on Churv

Hello everyone, I just want to speak up and share my thoughts here. Please hear me out. I no longer understand how the ROTC in our school is being managed. We students are aware of our responsibilities because no one wants to fail or be dropped, even if it’s just a minor subject. Yes, we understand that we cannot graduate if we do not complete ROTC, but I hope you also consider students who submit genuine excuse letters and medical certificates. No student would be foolish enough to pretend to be seriously ill. You rush us to submit excuse letters, yet when we do, you cannot even spare 30 seconds to read them. We also cannot predict when we will get sick, so how can we submit excuse letters three days in advance just to have them approved and be treated properly in front of you? And what happens to us? You are not the ones paying the hospital bills. You are not the parents who stay up all night to take care of us. Most of all, you are not the ones suffering and carrying the illness we are dealing with. When you say, “If you can’t handle it, just sit at the side,” do you really think sitting on the side can heal our sickness? We know our boundaries and responsibilities, but how can we attend formations if we force our bodies just to present ourselves in front of you? We are all human, just like you. We are students and cadets who understand how to empathize with one another. We are not robots—we are human beings who feel tired, drained, stressed, and exhausted during every formation. I hope you learn to show compassion toward your cadets because, just like you, we are simply trying to live our lives. In the end, we are all working hard and will likely earn the same minimum wage in the future. —ulol67 — @ulol67 on Churv

These recent discourses about the possible imposition of blended or online classes reveal only one thing — physical classes remain superior as the mode of learning these days. Every student has their own personal reasons and situations that might hinder them from fully focusing and actually learning in blended or online classes. Not every student has the geographical advantage, financial capacity and technological literacy to fullfil the needs of online and blended learning. On the other hand, it will be a waste to every CSUans that directed a fraction of their money to boarding houses, dorms, and others — might as well fully implement online classes if the goal to reduce financial burden to everyone which will be inconsiderate to others. More than that, with the advent of Artificial Intelligence — we will further accelerate the rusting of academic performances of each golden paddlers as evidenced by the system that lingered during pandemic times and its consequences. Physical classes provide incomparable hands-on teaching, ensure academic integrity, and proper assessment of student's performance that is lacking in an online setting. In a nutshell, online or blended classes is an inferior path given its effects and potential hindrances toward academic prowess for many golden paddlers. — @torturedpoet07 on Churv

From an outsider’s perspective, the original post did not come across as mere negativity, but rather as constructive criticism. In fact, some of the points raised were valid. Compared to previous productions, there were noticeable differences, and it is only natural for people to express their observations, especially when expectations are high. What is more concerning is not the criticism itself, but how it was received. As an officer, there is a certain level of responsibility that comes with the role, especially in terms of professionalism, composure, and openness to feedback. Responses like that can be disappointing, as they reflect not only on the individual but on the organization as a whole. Leadership is not just about executing plans, but also about how one handles pressure, feedback, and public perception. Growth comes from acknowledging lapses, listening to concerns, and responding with maturity. Not all criticism is meant to bring people down; sometimes, it exists to push standards higher and encourage improvement. I may not know the full story behind what happened, but one thing is certain, learning to accept criticism with humility and grace is essential. I grew up poor, but I was fortunate enough to have parents who, despite being teenagers at the time, never made me and my twin feel the burden of being underprivileged. Of course, I wouldn’t forget to give credit to my grandparents, who supported us and made sure we had milk to drink while our parents continued their studies. Growing up, I noticed the transition of our lives. From being poor to now belonging in the upper-middle class, I have seen the difference. Through God’s grace, my parents worked hard and were really strategic with money and assets, which is why we live the life we have now. But beyond faith and hard work, do you know what else my parents had? They had dreams. My grandparents planted those dreams in them: the belief that they were allowed to want more, that a comfortable and fulfilling life was not out of reach. To clarify, there is nothing wrong with simple dreams. Every dream is beautiful, and not every dream is about being rich and popular. But what matters is that someone, at some point, makes you believe that dreaming is possible. Because the dreams we carry shape the futures we dare to pursue. I, too, have big dreams. One of them is to build a non-profit organization that creates opportunities for people who were once in the same position I was in. But I’ve come to realize that for many, dreaming itself is a privilege. When you are poor, your days are consumed by survival. Your world can feel small, not because you lack potential, but because you were never shown how wide it could be. You begin to believe that you can only achieve so little, and when your vision is limited, your choices become limited too. This is why some teenagers start families at a young age. It’s not simply out of desire, but because it is what they believe to be a complete and fulfilling life. It is what is familiar. When you are not shown other paths, you cannot choose them. And that is what I want to change. I want to be someone who plants dreams in others the way my grandparents did for my parents. I want to show people that their lives can take different directions. They are allowed to choose, to explore, to dream. Because when people are given the chance to dream, they are also given the chance to decide. So what should I start doing? I actually need guidance from people on how I can make my dream possible. What tangible actions can I take to truly help people see that they have more options than the life they were born into? — @babynibiboy on

In light of the continuous increase in fuel prices, I respectfully urge the government to consider implementing practical and responsive measures to ease the burden on the public. One possible solution is to allow double backriding on motorcycles, particularly for students. For instance, if two students from [[CARAGA]] travel together, it would significantly reduce the need for multiple trips, thereby conserving fuel and minimizing expenses. Additionally, Instead of providing a fixed ₱2,000 subsidy to drivers, the government may consider offering an alternative system that benefits both drivers and commuters. For instance, commuters could be allowed to ride for free along designated routes, while drivers continue to operate their services as usual. At the end of the day, drivers would then receive compensation from the government through designated claiming centers such as the City Hall or barangay covered courts. This approach could help ease the financial burden on commuters while ensuring that drivers are still fairly compensated for their work. However, it is also important that proper monitoring and guidelines are established to prevent any form of abuse from both commuters and drivers. There is this chained message circulating in the socmed right now. And some CSUAN keep on sharing the same message without even fact checking. Just in case...Everyone, stay safe. Big day tomorrow. It’s official. Signed at 6:00am. It was even on TV. Mine really turned blue. Don't forget that tomorrow marks the start of the new Facebook rule (also known as Meta), which allows them to use your photos. Don't forget the deadline is today!!! -No specific date is mentioned, which makes the first paragraph false and misleading. There is no new Facebook rule or Meta policy change. To those sharing the message: what exactly turned blue? According to the show 60 Minutes: Just in case you missed it: a lawyer advised us to post this. The violation of privacy can be punished by law. NOTE: Facebook Meta is now a public entity. Every member must post a note like this. If you do not publish a statement at least once, it will be technically understood that you are allowing the use of your photos, as well as the information contained in your profile status updates. -According to CBS News' 60 Minutes, there are no verified interviews supporting these claims. Who's this lawyer anyway? Posting a message on your wall never enhances your privacy. It doesn't work. According to AI No, the phrasing "you are allowing the use of your photos, as well as the information contained in your profile status updates" is not a direct quote from Meta's terms of service or privacy policy. Key Terms Excerpts Meta's terms include clauses like: "Permission to use your name, profile picture, and information about your actions with ads... can be used next to or in connection with ads... without compensation." This covers profile info and photos for platform functions, sharing, and targeted ads, but users retain ownership and can delete content. Hoax Context Chain posts claiming to "deny permission" are ineffective because terms are agreed upon via account creation or updates, not overridden by reposts. No new rule matches this; recent changes focus on AI personalization or safety, not photo grabs. Check settings or official pages for controls. The Unspoken Debt I used to look at my hands rough from work, tired from the shift and feel proud, because I knew the money i earned was keeping you in a classroom I never got to sit in. I traded my own dreams for your degree. I was okay with being 'just' a high school graduate, as long as it meant you could be more. I gave you everything I had my time, my salary, and the best years of my youth, thinking I was investing in our future. ​But while I was working to pay for your reality, you were busy curated a fantasy. It hurts that after all the sacrifices, I was discarded because I didn't fit into the aesthetic of your feed. You compared my sweat to his style, and my hard-earned stability to your 'boybestfriend’s' social media presence. You measured my worth in likes and trends, forgetting that the very life you're living was built on the things I went without. ​I didn't lose a girlfriend I lost the person I thought was worth the struggle. And while you walk away with a diploma and a status, I walk away with the realization that I gave the world to someone who only wanted a filter. To you from a woman that I love from BSSW 2nd year. It’s strange how everything began with a confession on a platform like Freedom Wall and now, it finds its ending in the very same way. Since you’ve blocked me and closed that door completely, I’ll use this space to express the depth of my gratitude for you, and the weight of my regret that things had to end like this. The chances of you ever reading this without me mentioning your name are slim, unlike before but I still hold onto the quiet hope that somehow, through coincidence or fate, you or perhaps one of your classmates might come across this and unknowingly lead it to you. First and foremost, please attend your gala and the after-party. You deserve every moment of it especially all the awards and recognition you’ve rightfully earned. To you, [REDACTED_NAME] (I will call you [REDACTED_NAME]), I offer my sincerest apologies for the pain, the disappointments, and the resentment you carried throughout our years together. God knows I tried, in every way I understood how, to love you, to be patient with you, and to understand you. Yet I failed in ways that mattered most. I am deeply sorry that you had to endure that suffering alone that for five long months, your feelings were quietly fading while I remained unaware. I failed to notice the silence that spoke louder than words. I failed to tend to the small fractures in our relationship, not realizing they would one day become the reason it would break. If only I hadn’t filed for LOA, if only I hadn’t gone to Davao to work, if only I hadn’t left you here during the first semester perhaps our story would have unfolded differently. I carry with me a profound regret for leaving you behind without even seeking your thoughts first. It still aches to remember how you prepared for us buying a donut maker with the hope of building something together while I, blinded by my pursuit of independence, failed to measure the cost of my choices. I know that what I did crossed lines that should never have been crossed invading your privacy, overstepping boundaries, reaching out to your friend, and involving your ate in matters that should have remained between us. I was unraveling then, standing at the edge of my own sanity but even so, I know that does not absolve me. It never will. And for that, I am truly, deeply sorry. I also regret how I handled everything how I pleaded endlessly, how my pain only added to yours. You did not deserve that. [REDACTED_NAME], God knows the depth of my love for you but I grieve over my shortcomings, my incapability to fully process everything, and my failure to navigate the situation with the maturity it required. The poems I wrote for you were never empty words they were sincere, written long before AI was even a thought, even when you teased me about it. I will always stand in quiet support of you, even from afar. On the 30th, I will be there to watch your performance I already know you will be exceptional. I will remain somewhere in the crowd, unseen, but present. Even if we no longer walk the same path, I will show up. I was there before, cheering for your short play, and you triumphedI wouldn’t miss witnessing your brilliance again. You have always had your freedom, even from the very beginning of what we had. I encouraged you to embrace life, to live fully in your youth. But now that I no longer stand beside you, I cannot deny the quiet but loud anxiety that lingers within me. I know I have no right to ask anything of you anymore but if there is one thing I can still hope for, it is that you remain careful and kind to yourself in every path you choose. [REDACTED_NAME], you were the first person who made me understand what deep, genuine love truly feels like. Beyond the fleeting crushes and innocent affections of high school you were my first in everything. Every experience, every form of closeness, every piece of vulnerability you were my first. I may have been inexperienced, but my love for you was never shallow. I had envisioned so much for us, a future carefully imagined and deeply hoped for which is why it pains me to accept that our story has reached its end far sooner than I ever wished. But I understand you need this growth, and I cannot bear the thought of becoming a hindrance to the life you are meant to build. Perhaps our paths will never cross again. Still, I will strive to become a better person not for us anymore, but because of what we once had. I will no longer ask you to come back to me but I will humbly ask for your forgiveness, for every wound I caused. You were never at fault. You loved me in the best way you could, and our time together was, without question, filled with genuine happiness. If there is one thing I hope for, it is that you do not remember me with resentment but as someone who, despite his flaws, gave everything he had to love you the best way he knew how. This is where my chapter ends in the story of your life. I have learned plethora of things from you. I love you Anji’s Chari — @Anji_persona on Churv

He would insist on her going home early whenever she had a lot of schoolwork or wanted to go to church. She got annoyed because she felt he was being too controlling, even visiting her when he had school commitments. Despite his kindness, like buying her food and giving her allowance, she felt suffocated and decided to end their relationship. It was also kinda sketchy how he'd visit her in the evening, given he's a minor , she didn't want to be the one explaining things if something happened. Plus, he lived kinda far, which added to her worries. Things got more intense when the boy's mom called her one time, worried that her son hadn't come home yet. My friend panicked, thinking she was the last one with him, and worried because she was 19 and he was younger. She couldn't reach him and he was offline. A few minutes later, he texted her saying he was home and his phone was out of battery, which relieved her . Luckily nothing did, but it was a factor in her decision to end things. I’ve wanted to get this off my chest for a while na. I have this best friend of 6 years, and we have been nothing more than just friends. But I can’t help falling in love. We’ve been known to be very close since our 1st year, and I only saw him as a friend. A friend I can rely on, a friend I can trust, a friend I can laugh with, and bond. We would go home together, talk when there were no classes, help each other in academics, played games online together, bonded more because we were officers in the same organization, and etc. A lot of our mutual friends liked him, and at first I didn’t get the hype. What could they possibly see in him? [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] among mga friends “unsa diay naa [REDACTED_NAME] niya??” But now, I get it. He had this one admirer, which was a close friend of mine. I tried helping her, but it seems like he wasn’t ready daw. She then opened up to me that she had this kind of jealously towards me because she genuinely thought me and him were dating. I was so confused on what made her say that, [REDACTED_NAME] she then explained all the sweet things we did together. We would go on these “dates” where it was just the two of us, sweet noh? [REDACTED_NAME] as friends ra [REDACTED_NAME]. Our friends called it out and said “hoyyy [REDACTED_NAME] nohhh???” Which made me laugh, but that idea got stuck in my head. And then I started noticing the little details. He would buy me snacks, help me out, accompanied me, entertained me, and so much more, all that without having to ask. So na gets gyud nako ang iyang mga admirers—they were right. He just had that personality that draws you in, the kind of humor that kind make you smile, intelligence that can make you admire, leadership and kindness that makes you inspired. He was always there. He just understood me. And that was the part that bothered me. The truth is, I was so scared falling in love with my best friend. I didn’t want to ruin the friendship, and the sad part is that we’re graduating this year na. I remember opening up to a friend who asked me what was really going on with me and the guy, and I finally got to say it out loud that I have been just denying my feelings for him for years. She asked me why don’t I just confess, after all, I’m not an awkward person after rejection. But to ruin what we had? I can’t remember the exact conversation, but I had finally faced the truth—I had feelings for my best friend, and I kept on denying it. So, I decided to open up to our circle of friends—and they weren’t surprised. I remember telling them that I want to like him but there’s something pulling me back. He’s so sweet, and all of my friends agreed. Anyone can tell we just had this kind of chemistry. We’ve had our own partners over the years, siguro there were just times where we were both single and it felt like it was the right moment to risk the friendship. But it was always bad timing, idk why. But there was just those moments, the thought, of falling in love and trying something more with him. They (our friends) said they were always rooting for us to finally get together. But they also brought up another thing, his first love, who is also my best friend. It always seemed like he would crawl back to her, despite her moving on, we could just tell he was still healing, if not—just waiting. Another reality I had to face is that [REDACTED_NAME] iyang first love, and I’ve accepted that. I just don’t know what to do. I really thought na I’ve moved on and the thought of an “us” no longer exists. But the way we’re physically affectionate to each other, the way he still keeps doing those tiny efforts, the moments that keep happening as if there’s something more—I really can’t help it gyud. I can’t deny anymore that I am in love with my best friend. But what do I do? It’s too late to try something na. Do I just never tell him how I feel? Do I tell him before we part ways? Do I risk something? Do I ruin the friendship now? — @wendy1_ on Churv

This is long overdue, but my take about the debate "Are Transwomen considered as women?" A transwoman is NOT a woman Basic Biology aside, being a "woman" is not just a title, it is a multi century-old baggage. Throughout history, women are oppressed, seen as below men, and given little value. They rose up and obtained the equality they deserved. They did not choose this life, but they accepted it and fought to receive equality. Their strength, bravery, and courage is what makes them women. You were born a man, no matter how you view it, you were once a man. Yes, you may have faced discrimination for your way of life but it is a speck compared to what women experienced. You are called a transwoman because it encapsulates how you transitioned from being a man to someone resembling that of a woman. Yes, you changed parts of your body for the hopes of becoming a "real" woman, but remember that being a woman does not end with you having womanly parts, women are shaped and defined by centuries of oppression, bravery, and victory. This is something that you will never obtain and you cannot take from them. Actually, you insisting that you are a woman shows that even though you transitioned, you still carry the qualities of a man- that being demanding and close minded, just like the male-dominated oppressors of the past. You are a transwoman and will always be a transwoman. That is your identity and there is nothing wrong with that. We as lgbt, are already accepted by society, wag abusado. We have taken so much from the likeness of a woman, from the way they dress to their way of living. For once, as a show of respect and gratitude, let's leave the women something they can call their own, something that shaped their identity, something that isn't part of our own. Tl, dr: Being a women is an identity built through centuries of experiences, it is unjust to call yourself as one when you were not a part of these experiences. Your identity is that of a transwoman, you must accept that and build your identity from the ground up throughout the centuries to come. Do not take the identity of others, build your own identity from start to finish. The "T" in LGBTQ+ stands for Transsexuals, not Thieves. — @Zoroa on Churv

Sociology is not just a subject—it is a lens that changes the way you see the world. Once you begin studying it, ordinary things no longer feel ordinary. You start asking deeper questions: Why does poverty persist even when people work hard? Why do certain groups have more opportunities than others? Why do some social problems seem to repeat generation after generation? Sociology teaches us that many issues we see in society are not simply the result of individual choices but are shaped by systems, institutions, culture, and history. It trains the mind to look beyond the surface and understand the invisible forces that influence how people live, think, and interact in society. In the Philippines, this perspective is especially powerful. For example, when we see children selling sampaguita on the streets or students dropping out of school to help their families, it is easy to assume that their situation is purely personal. But sociology reveals a deeper story. It shows how historical inequality, economic structures, access to education, and even political decisions shape these realities. It helps us understand why certain communities remain trapped in cycles of poverty while others continue to progress. Once you begin to see these connections, you realize that social problems are rarely random—they are produced by patterns within society itself. For young Filipinos who truly want change, sociology becomes a starting point for awareness and responsibility. It challenges us to question what we once accepted as “normal” and pushes us to think critically about the society we are part of. While studying sociology alone may not immediately solve the problems of our nation, it opens our minds to the deeper roots of those problems. And when people begin to understand why problems exist, they are no longer passive observers—they become individuals capable of imagining solutions and participating in meaningful change. In this way, sociology does more than educate; it awakens a generation to see society clearly and to realize that change begins with understanding. averies Date Posted: 03/12/

As I continued reading [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] nako both God’s sorrow og iyang love. Some passages spoke of tearing, discipline, and judgment. Is college really hard, or have I simply underestimated myself? Is it just me or have you also felt that college feels more draining than it is "hard"? Like, I used to be a consistent 1st decile or below performing student to the point that it really infuriates me whenever someone places average or high expectations on me, in which even to this day it still does and my envy for others still persists. I worried a lot back in elementary about how I'd probably fail to graduate highschool considering I failed most of my entrance exams by then. At that point, I just accepted my fate and stopped studying or bothering to give the average effort in academics, and so, other students would avoid to have me as a group member. But somehow post pandemic, highschool suddenly became a whole lot easier than elementary and I passed all the entrance exams I took without studying. Then, I decided to go for a program which didn't align with my interests, skills, nor knowledge, but somehow to this day, I still am managing to do decently at least according to others who somehow think that grades define your intelligence. Now every single day feels like I'm just waiting for the requirements to pile up just for me to manage to cram them in less than a day or two. Like, it's gotten to a point where you just observe others whine about how hard some courses are then use that as a justification to cheat during exams but you ignore them because you don't even see the value in the so called academic integrity, and you didn't really work hard so there isn't really anything to gain from reporting them. They say that college humbles you, but somehow my nonexistent self-esteem since preschool somehow feels like it's beginning to form. Have some of you guys felt this phenomena as well? Should I just try to be a perfectionist instead of rotting? - random rant from the other side Entry Date: 3/1/ 8:42:07

Would you sell your soul twice in order to spend a fraction of a second... As these hands of time eternally rotate, flowing generously like sand poured down. Would you chase the echoes of a heartbeat long vanished, or grasp at shadows that dissolve before your eyes? Each grain falling whispers the love you lost, and yet, some part of you still reaches... Fragile like a vase, knitted with yarn — these were the instruments. Struck and plucked by shadows, humming melodies of what could never stay. You were a sin made to flourish in the garden of the snake, carving what molded to be this fruit of forbiddenness. A curse entwined in your flavor, binding me to your shadowed sweetness. I trace the edges of your absence, fingers brushing through a canvas still wet with your memory. Strokes of ruin and desire, layered endlessly — and still, I reach, though the pigments of you smudge beneath my touch. - The Loyal Soul Entry Date: 2/26/ 11:25:53

LONG POST AHEAD: MY THOUGHTS ON POLITICS RIGHT NOW. Warning : This is just my personal political opinion, and I respect that other people may have different views. I’m not trying to attack anyone, but I just want to share my thoughts and questions about what’s happening in our politics right now. ‎ ‎ ‎So Hiii, everyone. Lately, I’ve been feeling really bothered and curious about the people who are still blindly siding with VP S*r*h Duterte. It’s hard to understand the level of loyalty some people have, especially when you look closely and ask yourself: has she actually done anything significant that warrants this kind of devotion? ‎ ‎It’s no secret that she has officially signaled her intent to run for President in . But the big question we all need to ask is: CAN SHE PERFORM WELL IN ? DOES SHE TRULY DESERVE TO LEAD THIS COUNTRY? BEING A "DUTERTE" SHOULD NOT BE AN AUTOMATIC QUALIFICATION FOR THE HIGHEST OFFICE IN THE LAND. ‎ ‎We all know her father, former President [REDACTED_NAME], had a very distinct and impactful term. While I personally don’t agree with his some methods, especially regarding the "War on Drugs with no due process," one i can’t deny HE HAD A CLEAR, FORCEFUL AGENDA. However, WE CANNOT ASSUME SRH WILL RUN THE COUNTRY THE SAME WAY. Just because they share the same blood doesn't mean they share the same capability; there is no guarantee her leadership would mirror the parts of his presidency that people actually liked. ‎ ‎Right now, it feels like S*r*h is mostly famous for being a "political heir." She has inherited a massive fan base because of her father, but inheritance isn't the same as earning it through service. It feels more like a brand name being passed down RATHER THAN A LEADER RISING BECAUSE OF HER OWN GROUNDBREAKING ACHIEVEMENTS ON A NATIONAL LEVEL. ‎ ‎One of the biggest red flags has been the controversy surrounding her confidential funds. People were shocked to learn how hundreds of millions of pesos were being requested and spent with very little transparency. When the public asks for an explanation on where the money went, they are often met with silence or defensive remarks rather than a clear breakdown of how those taxes served the people. ‎ ‎Then there is the issue of her children’s book, "[REDACTED_NAME]." She proposed a P10 million budget for to print and distribute 200,000 copies of a book she authored herself. Why should taxpayers fund a book that features the Vice President's face and story, especially WHEN OUR EDUCATION SYSTEM IS STRUGGLING WITH ACTUAL TEXTBOOK SHORTAGES? ‎ ‎What makes it even more questionable is that, despite the massive budget request, I haven't actually seen this book in a single child classroom. If millions are being allocated for "educational materials" authored by the VP, where are they? It feels like another project that looks good on paper but doesn't actually reach the students who need help the most. ‎ ‎Looking at her current performance, it’s honestly hard to find the "wins." Many are left wondering what her actual contributions have been. Instead of seeing her on the ground solving local issues or fixing the schools during her time at DepEd, we often hear about her frequent travels abroad. While international relations are important, a leader's primary duty is to the people at home. ‎ ‎In the end, We need to stop voting based on nostalgia and start holding officials accountable for their actual output. Let’s look at the track record, the transparency, and the results not just the name on the poster. Our country deserves a president who earns the position through hard work, not just through a famous surname. ‎ AGAIN: S*R*H IS NOT THE SAME AS HER FATHER. ‎MAONG VOTE WISELY SA , GUYS MALOUY SAB MOS PILIPINAS ‎ - FOR THE FUTURE Entry Date: 2/25/ 11:53:59

When Leadership is Questioned There comes a moment in every academic community when silence becomes heavier than words. Not because conflict is desired, but because clarity is needed. Recently, conversations have surfaced—not out of rebellion, but out of concern. Students have begun to ask questions. Questions about direction. About priorities. About whether student leadership is truly aligned with the needs of the people it represents. And perhaps that is where leadership is truly tested. Leadership is not proven during applause. It is proven during scrutiny. It is easy to lead when everyone agrees. It is more difficult when people begin to ask, “Why?” Yet that very question is not a threat. It is participation. It is engagement. It is the evidence that students care enough to think critically about the system they are part of. In academic spaces, questioning is not disrespect—it is a reflection of the education we are taught to value. We are trained to analyze, to evaluate, to seek justification. When students raise concerns about planning, impact, or transparency, they are not tearing the student body down. They are asking it to rise higher. True leadership does not silence concerns; it welcomes them. It does not respond defensively; it responds responsibly. A leader confident in their purpose does not fear accountability, because accountability strengthens trust. There is nothing wrong with ambition. There is nothing wrong with wanting to conduct programs, to reach outward, to expand influence. But expansion without reflection can feel distant. Growth must remain connected to those at the core—the very students who form the foundation of this academic community. The heart of leadership is not control. It is stewardship. It is remembering that every decision carries weight. That every plan affects real people. That representation means listening even when the message is uncomfortable. This moment is not about division. It is about direction. Communities grow when dialogue is open. Leaders grow when feedback is acknowledged. Student leadership grows when trust is rebuilt through clarity and action. The question is not whether leaders will be challenged. They will. The real question is how they respond when they are. Because in the end, leadership is not defined by position—but by the courage to listen, the humility to reflect, and the integrity to act. - - Ur constructive commenter Entry Date: 2/25/ 4:19:29

A Patient Thought Wrote this abruptly, to clear my clouded mind. "Are you holding on because it still feels like love, or because letting go would mean admitting it changed? When you reread old conversations at midnight, are you searching for them, or for the version of you that felt chosen? If they return only in memories, will you keep building a home out of echoes? And if someone new knocks gently at your door, will you answer — or compare their warmth to a fire that has already burned out? Is loyalty always noble, or can it become a cage dressed as devotion? How long will you call it patience when it has quietly turned into postponing your own joy? Tell me, are you protecting a love that still lives, or preserving a story that has already ended?" Regardless of these thoughts, I'm here. I chose this. I want you, and only you. I'll be waiting for the next sunrise, the next shooting star, and the next time you look at me full of love — not like what we used to have, but with a new profound meaning to the love we can both give. A love that lasts, and perseveres in the most difficult. I miss you. - A changed lover Entry Date: 2/24/ 22:10:16

White Ceiling The footsteps fade first. Then my heartbeat follows. Longing wraps around me like a heavy coat I never chose to wear. The place is quiet—too quiet. The kind of silence that does not comfort, but warns. Cold wind brushes my cheek, and a shiver crawls down my spine. Ahead stands a tall building, pale and unmoving. We walk toward it without speaking. I clutch my pillow tightly against my chest, as if it can protect me from something I cannot yet name. By the time we reach the ground floor, dread has already settled deep inside me. What was once silent soon drowns in distant screams. We pass room after room until we reach one painted in perfect white. The walls are spotless—but the faces inside are not. Fear, pain, confusion—emotions stain them darker than any color ever could. Then I see her. A girl lying on a bed. My breath catches when I notice the bracelet on her wrist. It matches the one I once had. My eyes stay fixed on her. Slowly, her eyes open. For a moment, our gazes lock, and something unspoken passes between us. She smiles. Her face feels familiar, yet I cannot place it. Her head is bare, no hair framing her features, yet there is something striking about her presence. My heart pounds wildly, like a horse racing toward the finish line. I force myself to smile back, but tears slip down my cheeks before I can stop them. She rises slowly and walks toward the small window. I watch her every step. Then she turns and stretches out her hand toward me. I hesitate—but I take it. Her touch is cold. Too cold. It pulls memories from a place I have tried to bury. My past flickers like a nightmare I cannot escape. And then, suddenly, she laughs. Not a joyful laugh. A sharp, unsettling one. I pull my hand away. At that exact moment, I hear my name being called. The woman who had been walking beside me earlier—who promised to guide me to the library—calls me again. I turn away from the girl and follow the voice. We stop at a door. Inside, the “library” is nothing like I imagined. Instead of books and silence, it is filled with cries and broken laughter. Some people are weeping. Others are laughing at nothing at all. My eyes drift to the sign on the door. Room 14 — Asylum. The word hits me like ice against skin. Fear replaces whatever curiosity I once had. The woman who brought me here begins to walk away. Panic rises in my chest. I run after her and grab her arm— But when she turns around, it is no longer the woman. It is her. The girl from the white room. She smiles again, but this time it is twisted—threatening. “Welcome, best friend,” she says softly. “Now, you will take my place. Farewell.” Her words slice through me. “You shaved my head because you didn’t want competition in the beauty contest. You took my hair—my pride, my weapon. You pushed me into madness. You brought me here and stole my freedom. Now it is your turn. From the very beginning, you were the one losing your sanity.” The nightmare crashes over me like a wave I cannot fight. Now, every day, all I see is a white ceiling. The cries echo inside my heart. The past replays over and over again. And on that blank, endless ceiling, I paint the memories I cannot erase—the darkness of yesterday that refuses to fade. - @yans✍🏼 Entry Date: 2/24/ 21:15:08

The middle class is indeed the new poor I thought that this was an exageration until I realized our situation. VAT aside, more or less 15% percent of a middle class' income goes to the government due to taxes. For context, our household has an income of around 100k per month before taxes and around 82k after taxes. That is 18k in taxes PER MONTH and then multiply that by the number of middle class incomers and you'll get not just millions, but billions per month. The disappointing part is that, the middle class is neglected. Without researching, think about what are the benefits this tax bracket is getting without the other tax brackets getting? Now think about what the lower class income are getting? 4Ps, [REDACTED_NAME], Scholarships, and all other stuffs. I' m not saying I'm against it, my point is that we are the largest tax generators so we should at least get our money's worth! They can do both naman-provide for those in need and give us benefits. Did you also know that I was denied a scholarship? Mind you that the scholarship was advertised as "for all" but when they learned about our household income, they denied my application. Like bro, 82k a month is not that much when there are 4 kids in school (2 private) without the government's help. Also, during the pandemic, we did not get any assistance [REDACTED_NAME] "dako mana silag sweldo, afford mana nila, daghan mana silag kwarta" yeah, like our lives did not go on a hiatus during that time. Then I'll hear some people say "manglaag [REDACTED_NAME] pay out naman sa ing ani ing [REDACTED_NAME] assistance" or a post saying "yes release [REDACTED_NAME] scholarship [REDACTED_NAME]". [REDACTED_NAME] all [REDACTED_NAME] gyud no, because I've never tried that even when my parents are working hard not just to provide for us but also for the government. - Disappointingly yours, [REDACTED_NAME] Date: 2/24/ 19:53:18

Undescribable feelings Good day to all, please don't judge me basing on this statement. I am so confused rn about myself and my decisions in life. First of all, I'm someone who grew up in a strict and gaslit environment which makes me super sensitive and always doubtful of my actions/decisions. This started in high school and continues up until now that I'm in college. They are so strict to the point where it affects my relationship with them. I no longer want to confront them about my everday struggles, about my relationship with my friends, about my feelings and my future plans(in fact, I really don't have any cause I became dependent on them), afraid that they'll be mad at me and gaslight me again. I got depressed, no one knows. I never told anyone, it's only me and my consultant. Despite knowing my mental state, I still followed what they want for me. Afraid I would disappoint them, I never tried to go against them, I never get the chance to enjoy being a teen, I'm fully submerged with studies to reach their expectations. I never go out of the house unless I was told. I never go out with my friends or date anyone. I've always imagined what it feels like to be in love. I gave up the thought and proceeded to pursue their dream for me, and got to college. All went well. Then, there's this man. He makes me feel comfortable and safe, something that I've never felt with anyone else. There's this connectivity that I can never describe, he appreciates me for who I am, he listens without complaints, he protects me like I'm some kind of antique and he gives effort in any ways. I've been thinking, is this what they call love? If yes, then please don't let it disappear. Please let it choose me and maybe I'll choose it too. But then, reality hits. I know "they" wouldn't agree, i know that all of this will end because of "them", but what about my happiness? What about my feelings? When can I ever get MY own choice? WHAT ABOUT ME? Then something hit me again. Feelings? Am I like this because of the feeling? Am I really in love or am I only enjoying the attention that is given to me? Am I only infatuated? I DON'T KNOW ANYMORE! My sanity feels vague. It all feels like I'm living another person's body where I have no control. - BlueSky Entry Date: 2/24/ 10:10:20

A Love So Steady, So Calm Yet So Scary A love that is steady feels like peace. No chaos. No loud fights. No dramatic highs and lows. Just calm. Just comfort. Just knowing someone is there. It feels safe — like you don’t have to question where you stand. Like you’ve already survived the storms and now you’re just... steady. But sometimes, that calm becomes scary. Because when things are too quiet, you start to wonder — Are we okay, or are we just used to each other? Are we still in love, or are we just comfortable? Are we choosing each other, or just staying because we’ve been here for so long? There are no butterflies anymore, no late-night endless conversations, no excitement to see each other every day. Just routine. Just silence. Just distance that doesn’t scream — but slowly grows. And that’s what makes it scary. Not the fighting. Not the drama. But the quiet fading. A steady love is beautiful. But it still needs effort. It still needs time. It still needs two people who choose each other — not just out of habit, but out of intention. Because love isn’t supposed to be loud... but it’s not supposed to feel lonely either. - Entry Date: 2/23/ 20:56:00

Certificate of Disability (UPDATE) Here’s the link for the context. I’ve been so disappointed with some of the comments. No amount of education can save you if you choose to be ignorant. Times have changed and so should you. I have great news. Tomorrow, I will be processing my PWD ID. The [REDACTED_NAME] from the City Hall gave me a certificate of disability. The last requirement left before I process. Thank you to the people who were nice and helpful. I’m just a 19-year-old girl still navigating through the system all by myself. I needed guidance and support and to those who were empathetic of my situation, thank you. I appreciate you. Thank you for not being a judgmental person. As for those people who were very bitter by my diagnosis, please know that being harsh in the wrong time and place reflects who you REALLY are as a person. Depression, specifically Persistent Depressive Disorder, is a long-term illness. I’ve had depressive episodes that had disabled me from doing daily activities. Depression IS a disability and it is recognized globally as a disability. I don’t make up my diagnosis. I went and spent actual money just so I can get better. And for me to acknowledge the fact that I need help shows courage not a lot can muster. I did not get a PWD to “outsmart the system.” I come from a lower-class family and you already know, a lot of things are expensive especially medicine for depression. Please, if you don’t understand the illness, be nice. That’s the least that you could do. People don’t understand depression unless they’ve been through it themselves. It’s very hard to fight my thoughts as I’ve fought this battle silently since . Now, I’m finally getting the help I need. Thanks for reading. - God's sleepiest soldier Entry Date: 2/23/ 4:40:12

HELLO GENTLE READER I don’t know what battles you’re fighting right now. Maybe you’re tired. Maybe you’re silently breaking while still showing up every day. Maybe you’re smiling outside but crying inside. And maybe, just maybe, you’re already thinking of giving up. But please... don’t. This season of your life is not here to destroy you. It’s here to shape you. The pain you feel right now? It won’t last forever. The struggles you’re facing? They are not permanent. You are stronger than the situation that’s trying to break you. It’s okay to feel weak. It’s okay to rest. But don’t quit. Fight for your dreams. Fight for your peace. Fight for the future version of yourself who will one day look back and say, “I’m glad I didn’t give up.” Even if it’s slow progress. Even if it’s one small step at a time. Even if no one sees your effort. Keep going.You survived 100% of your worst days. And you will survive this too. Hold on. The story isn’t over yet. To the one I let go Ang ironic no? [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] People probably think I’m okay. After all, it was my decision. [REDACTED_NAME] what they don’t see is how hard that decision was. I didn’t end us because I didn’t love you. I ended us because I thought it was the right thing to do even if it hurt. And now here I am, still healing from something I chose. I miss the routine. I miss the comfort. I miss the version of us that felt safe and familiar. Sometimes I question myself, sometimes I stand firm in my decision. Both can exist at the same time. And even though you’ve already moved on to someone else, I’m still here trying to piece myself back together. Not because I want you back, but because healing isn’t a race. Just because you found someone new doesn’t mean my feelings disappeared overnight. I’m not writing this to go back. I’m writing this because I’m learning that choosing yourself doesn’t mean you won’t feel pain. Letting go doesn’t mean you stop caring in an instant. Maybe one day I’ll fully move forward without this heaviness. For now, I’m just being honest because I know healing takes time, even for the one who walked away. - — Still learning to let go 🤍 Entry Date: 2/22/ 8:35:49

A simple message and advice to my co-CSUans who were cheated, rejected, and still finding genuine love. When there’s contentment, love grows. Love does not grow in pressure. It grows in peace. In my experience, our love became stronger not because we had everything, but because we chose to be content with what we had and who we were at that moment. Choosing a partner who is kontento kung unsa ka ug unsa pa lang ang inyong makaya is one of the greatest blessings in a relationship. It means you are loved not for your potential alone, not for what you might become someday, but for who you already are. There is no constant pressure to prove yourself. No silent competition. No endless comparison. Just two people appreciating what is present. Acceptance plays a big role. Acceptance sa imong partner — sa iyang strengths, iyang flaws, iyang limitations, ug even sa current season sa inyong life. Dili pa man [REDACTED_NAME] naa [REDACTED_NAME]. Dili pa siguro perfect ang inyong situation. But when both of you are content and grateful for what is already there, love becomes lighter. It becomes safer. Contentment does not mean settling for less or not dreaming big. It means you grow together without resentment. You build without blaming. You pursue more in life, but you do it side by side, thankful for what you already have. To my co-students who are busy finding genuine love: [REDACTED_NAME] ninyo i-rush. Love is not a race, and it is not something you force just to keep up with others. Focus first on becoming whole, grounded, and content with who you are. The right love will not pressure you to change who you are just to be accepted. Instead, it will meet you where you are and grow with you. Look for someone who chooses you even on ordinary days, not only when things are exciting. Someone who understands your struggles as a student, your limitations, and your dreams. Genuine love is patient, understanding, and kind — especially during seasons when you are still figuring life out. Remember: the love meant for you will not make you anxious or insecure. It will bring peace, reassurance, and growth. When there is contentment, there is peace. When there is peace, love has space to grow. 🌿 - Loved and Contented Entry Date: 2/21/ 18:56:03

I was the top. When I received my HIV result and it came out reactive, I felt like my world stopped. I was devastated, and the regret was overwhelming. We didn’t use protection because he told me it wouldn’t feel good, and I let myself be convinced. We were both carried away in the moment, and I chose pleasure over safety. Now I’m left facing the consequences of that decision. The regret is heavy. The “what ifs” don’t stop. I’m not sharing this to scare anyone or to ask for pity. I’m sharing this because I don’t want anyone else to feel the kind of fear and pain I felt when I saw that result. Please protect yourself. Don’t let anyone pressure you. A few minutes of hesitation could save you from a lifetime of “I wish I didn’t.” - — (⁠╯⁠(⁠╰⁠,⁠) Entry Date: 2/21/ 6:05:01

With utmost respect, we would like to formally request the extension of the suspension of classes in our university due to the continuous heavy rainfall and flooding currently affecting our area. In the past few days, our community has been experiencing persistent heavy rains brought by weather disturbances as monitored by the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA). These weather conditions have resulted in significant flooding in several areas near and around our residences. Many roads have become impassable, public transportation has been limited, and some students are unable to travel safely to and from the university. The situation poses serious safety concerns for students, faculty members, and university personnel. Flooded streets, slippery roads, and possible landslides increase the risk of accidents and health hazards. Moreover, some families are currently managing flood-related concerns in their homes, which makes it difficult for students to fully focus on their academic responsibilities at this time. In light of these circumstances, we respectfully appeal for your kind consideration in extending the suspension of classes until the weather improves and floodwaters subside. We firmly believe that prioritizing the safety and well-being of the university community is of utmost importance. First, I want you to know this: what you’re feeling is normal. Being proud and jealous at the same time doesn’t make you a bad person. It makes you human. It means you care. Sometimes, when others succeed, it shines a light on our own insecurities. And it hurts. But let me tell you something important: Their success is not your failure. Their path is theirs. Your path is yours. You are not behind. You are not less. You are simply in your own season. Right now, maybe you are in the season of building, not harvesting. One day, all the struggles you’re facing right now will make sense. All the hard moments, the silent battles, the self-doubt—you will look back and realize they made you stronger than you ever imagined. For now, just breathe. Rest if you need to. Hello, gentle reader. It’s been a while. I don’t know what season of life you’re in right now. Maybe you’re single and learning how to enjoy your own company. Maybe you’re in a relationship, trying your best to make it work. Or maybe you’re somewhere in between healing, waiting, hoping. But wherever you are, I hope you remember this "your relationship status does not define your value". To the single ones there is nothing wrong with you. Being alone is not the same as being lonely. This season is not a punishment it is preparation. It is the time where you get to know yourself deeply, build your dreams boldly, and love yourself fully. Don’t rush love just because you feel left behind. The right love will never require you to shrink yourself just to fit. And to those who are in a relationship I hope it is gentle. I hope it is healthy. I hope it feels safe. Love should not confuse you every day. It should not make you question your worth. Real love supports your growth, respects your individuality, and chooses you even on ordinary days. Remember, love is not about losing yourself in someone it’s about growing together without forgetting who you are. At the end of the day, whether single or taken, you are already complete. You are already enough. The right people will add peace, not chaos. They will add clarity, not confusion. So don’t settle. Don’t compare your timeline with others. And don’t let the world pressure you into believing that being loved by someone else is more important than loving yourself. Love will come. And when it does, may it find you whole not empty, not desperate, but secure and strong. Until then, keep becoming the person you’re proud of... - Miss [REDACTED_NAME]💗 Entry Date: 2/15/ 7:56:27

Person who wants to achieve many things I have a lot of things I want to do and experience, but I don't know where to start. Like I want to do this, but I also want to do that at the same time. It's really frustrating, tbh, and it's also draining. But then I realize I can just do the things that I want to do one at a time. Because I asked myself, why are you acting like you're running out of time? Why are you so eager to do all those things at the same time when you know yourself you can't do it? I contemplated, digging into myself for the right answer. But then, there is no right answer—the only thing we have is the suitable and better answer. I was scared for the future, because who knows? I might be dead by then. But I think that makes life so exciting yet scary, because you don't know the future. You don't know the future! Read it again, and think of it deeply. I believe there is no such thing as shallow in this world; everything is deep and needs understanding. And it's enough for me to say " I guess this is not a bad life after all". - Grammatically incorrect Entry Date: 2/14/ 09:29:34

to fsuu np mae... To You, There are so many things I’ve rehearsed in my head, yet none of them ever seem enough when it comes to you. First, thank you. Thank you for the six years that shaped parts of me I didn’t even know existed. Thank you for the memories that still live quietly in the corners of my mind, the laughter that echoed too loudly, the petty fights that somehow turned into late-night talks, the comfortable silences, and the way we could sit across from each other carrying the world’s weight but still share one small smile. Some connections go deeper than labels. What we had was never just “us” as lovers. We were companions, rivals, protectors, critics, comforters. At times, we felt like siblings, teasing, clashing, knowing exactly which buttons to push and exactly how to soothe each other after. We fought in ways that felt like storms, yet somehow, after every downpour, we found ourselves sitting at the same table again. No matter how heavy the conflicts were, they never truly untied whatever it was that bound us. It’s strange how two people can collide so much and still remain tethered by something softer than words something invisible, but undeniable. And maybe that’s why this hurts the way it does. Six years is not something the heart forgets overnight. It is not something I can simply fold away like old letters and pretend it meant nothing. You became familiar to me, your presence, your habits, even your silence. And now that you are building something new with someone else, I am trying to learn how to quietly step back without breaking inside. This is where I need to be honest. Please, for now, stop viewing me. Stop checking my socmed. Stop giving me mixed signals or subtle motives that make my heart pause and wonder. I say this not out of anger, not out of bitterness, and not because I want to create a negative distance between us. I say this because I am still healing. I cannot move forward if I keep feeling your shadow behind me. I cannot find my own peace if I keep sensing that part of you is still watching. It makes letting go heavier. It makes acceptance harder. And as much as I care about what we had, I need to choose my own peace this time. This ending does not erase what we were. It does not make our memories fake. It does not turn our love into something meaningless. It simply reminds me that sometimes love is real and still not enough to make two people stay. So I am letting go gently. With love. With respect. With gratitude for the version of us that held on for as long as we could, even when it was hard. For now, I am giving myself time. Time to grow without looking back every second. Time to rebuild parts of me that were intertwined with you. Time to learn how to breathe without wondering if you’re still there. Maybe someday, when the ache is no longer sharp and the memories no longer sting, we can look back without pain just appreciation. But for now, please let me have my quiet. Let me have my healing. Let me have my peace. Thank you for the love we shared. And goodbye not with hatred, but with a soft kind of sorrow. - — Someone who once loved you deeply Entry Date: 2/14/ 7:21:21

LOVE LETTER To My One and Only, The Beginning of Us I’ve been sitting here for a long time trying to find the right words to capture everything I feel for you. How do you fit a lifetime of devotion into a few pages? How do you explain the way the world shifted the moment you entered my life? Before you, everything felt like it was in grayscale. The Way You Change My World It isn't just the big moments that make me love you; it’s the quiet ones. It’s the way you look when you’re focused on something you’re passionate about.
