When the world gets too loud and the stress of life starts to weigh me down, you are the person I turn to. Just the sound of your voice acts like an anchor, pulling me back to where I belong. You listen to me in a way that no one else ever has. You see the parts of me that I try to hide from everyone else—the insecurities, the fears, the messy bits—and instead of walking away, you lean in closer. You love me not just for my strengths, but for my flaws, and that is the greatest gift anyone has ever given me. Our Shared Journey Think about everything we’ve been through together. [Spend 200-300 words here describing a specific challenge you overcame together or a favorite trip you took. Talk about the "us against the world" feeling you had during that time.] Through the highs and the lows, my favorite place has remained the same: right by your side. We’ve built a language of our own—inside jokes that no one else understands, a shorthand of looks and touches that says "I love you" without a single word being spoken. Every memory we make is a new brick in the house we are building together, a foundation that I know will never crumble. The Future I See When I look ahead, I don’t see a life of perfection, but I see a life of us. I see us growing old together, our faces etched with the lines of a thousand smiles we shared. I see us navigating the storms that life will inevitably throw our way, knowing that as long as we are holding hands, we can’t be knocked down. I want to be there for your greatest triumphs and your hardest days. I want to be the person who cheers the loudest when you succeed and the one who holds you when you feel like you’ve failed. I want to learn every version of you that exists as the years go by. I want to love the person you are today, and I want to fall in love all over again with the person you will become in ten, twenty, or forty years. My Promise to You So, here is my vow to you. I promise to never take you for granted. I promise to keep choosing you, every single morning, even on the days when things are hard. I promise to listen, to grow, and to always be your biggest fan. I promise to protect the heart you’ve entrusted to me with everything I have. You are my best friend, my partner in crime, and the love of my life. You are the best thing that has ever happened to me, and I will spend the rest of my days trying to make you feel as special, as seen, and as loved as you make me feel. She Cheated on Me for Her — Then Cheated on Her for Me We started young. High school love—the kind that feels innocent and forever. Almost six years together. Six years of growing up side by side, dreaming about the future, believing we were solid. I was the overthinker. I loved deeply, worried deeply, and stayed even when it hurt.�She was my person. But during those years, she cheated on me. Not once. Not twice. I lost count. And every time, I forgave her. I thought love meant understanding. I thought staying proved how much I cared. Looking back now, I know I was just afraid to lose her. When college came, everything changed. We went to different schools. She was taking engineering at [CARAGA], and I kept telling myself to be patient. Engineering is hard. Stressful. Demanding. So I understood when she was busy. I understood when she was tired. I understood when she had no time. I always visited her in [CARAGA]. I always showed up. Until I felt it—she was changing. There was a friend.�Just a friend, she said. When I confronted her, chaos followed. Our relationship turned toxic. She told me I was overthinking again, and because I loved her, I believed her. I convinced myself I was the problem. During Sangka Days, I went to see her. I waited. She never even faced me. Her reason? She was in the library—studying —with her “friend.” That was when my doubts started eating me alive. Months passed like that. Sleepless nights. Endless questions. Silent prayers. I asked God for a reason to stop, because I didn’t have the strength to walk away on my own. Then the truth showed itself. Videos.�Pictures.�Conversations. It was never just friendship. She had a rs while I was still holding on. That broke something in me. I ended the relationship, even when she denied cheating. I didn’t need her words anymore. I had already seen enough. Months after the breakup, I heard she was courting the girl she cheated with. And surprisingly, I felt no anger. I just wanted peace. Months passed. She came back. Messages turned into visits. She came to our house. She cooked for me. We played sports together. Slowly, she found her way back into my life. After classes, she would message me. On days she had no classes, she’d invite me out. The lines blurred again. I asked her many times if she and that girl were already together. I told her honestly: If you are, I’ll step away.�She always said no. Sometimes she said, “[REDACTED_NAME] man [REDACTED_NAME].” So I believed her—again. Until I saw the other girl’s story.�Until my friends from [CARAGA] confirmed it. They were already in a relationship. To that girl, I’m sorry. I didn’t know. That was the moment I stopped replying. No more explanations. No more chances. I chose respect—something she never gave either of us. It’s ironic, isn’t it? She cheated on me for her.�Then cheated on her for me. But this time, I’m done.�No more forgiveness.�No more overthinking. Goodbye. - wlw Entry Date: 2/12/ 19:15:36

When friendship turns toxic Not all friendships are meant to last forever and that’s okay. Some friends uplift you, while others slowly drain your peace, confidence, and energy. A toxic friend is not always loud or obvious. Sometimes they are the ones who invalidate your feelings, compete instead of support, or only stay when it benefits them. I learned that protecting your mental and emotional well-being is not selfish, it is necessary. Choosing distance is not hatred, it is self-respect. Real friends help you grow, not shrink. They listen, not judge. They stay, not just when it’s easy, but when it matters. To anyone who feels guilty for walking away from a toxic friendship, you are not wrong for choosing peace. You deserve relationships that are healthy, sincere, and safe. I haven’t done it yet, but soon I will. Dool na. - Justobserving Entry Date: 2/12/ 9:24:53

Sabta Lately Ive been hearing things travel in circles.. stories moving from person to person, yet somehow never reaching me directly. Its a strange system... people have plenty to say when Im not around, but none of it seems to exist when I am. I dont run after rumors, and I dont perform for whispers. If theres an issue involving me, I prefer clarity over noise. Were all walking the same campus breathing the same air and passing the same halls. It doesnt take much effort to speak to someone directly if theres something real to address. If Ive done something that bothered anyone, or if theres a misunderstanding being passed around, Im open to hearing it face to face. One honest conversation solves more than a hundred quiet comments ever will. Dragging my name through corners just wastes time and makes simple matters heavier than they need to be. I wont throw accusations or pretend to know who started what. But I dont stay unaware forever either. Eventually, things surface. And when they do, Id rather meet them directly, with calm and clarity. If you have something to say, step forward. If not, then let the noise die where it started. Either way, Ill get to the truth. Campuses arent that big, and paths cross naturally. So if theres something behind my name, well talk about it properly, and once. Im not here for drama. Im here for clarity, and I always find it. - See you around campus Entry Date: 2/7/ 12:30:31

Dear sen. [REDACTED_NAME] I am condemning the statement of marcoleta in Senate hearing last day. How can you say that we should give up or surrender the KIG(Kalayaan Group of Islands) to china because KIG is not part of our EEZ(Exclusive Economic Zone), yes KIG is not part of our [REDACTED_NAME] because KIG is more than 200 nautical miles from our baselines but we must remember even china claim the south China sea, KIG and Filipino people are the first inhabitants in the islands and the administration of [REDACTED_NAME]. Release a presidential decree, while [REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME]. Also released a executive order, lastly this administration of pres [REDACTED_NAME] Passed the two landmark laws about the archipelagic sea lines act and the Philippine maritime zone act. And also in this current admin release Executive order to create a council about west Philippines sea. To contextualize my claim. Heres the reference. Administration of Pres. [REDACTED_NAME] ([REDACTED_NAME] 11, ) Presidential Decree No. : This is the foundational law that formally annexed the Kalayaan Island Group. It declared the area as part of Philippine territory based on "history, indispensable need, and effective occupation." It also: Defined the specific geographic coordinates of the KIG. Established the area as a distinct municipality under the Province of Palawan. Vested initial governance in the Secretary of National Defense due to the state of emergency at the time. Presidential Decree No. : Issued on the same day, this decree established the Philippine Exclusive Economic Zone ([REDACTED_NAME]), extending 200 nautical miles from the baselines. This provided the legal basis for the Philippines' sovereign rights over the resources in the waters surrounding the Kalayaan islands. Lastly, the Municipality of Kalayaan also released Executive order. Administration of Pres. [REDACTED_NAME]. Administrative Order No. 29 () Issued by President [REDACTED_NAME], this order is often confused with an Executive Order. Naming the West Philippine Sea: It officially named the maritime areas on the western side of the archipelago—including the waters around, within, and adjacent to the Kalayaan Island Group—as the West Philippine Sea. Mapping: It directed the National Mapping and Resource Information Authority (NAMRIA) to publish official maps using this designation. Administration of Pres. [REDACTED_NAME] The Philippines recently passed two landmark laws (signed November 7, ) to cement its maritime claims: Republic Act No. (Philippine Maritime Zones Act): This is a cornerstone law that defines the various maritime zones of the Philippines—including internal waters, archipelagic waters, territorial sea, contiguous zone, Exclusive Economic Zone ([REDACTED_NAME]), and continental shelf—strictly in accordance with UNCLOS and the Arbitral Award. It explicitly names the waters on the western side of the archipelago as the West Philippine Sea. Republic Act No. (Philippine Archipelagic Sea Lanes Act): This law establishes specific sea lanes and air routes for the continuous and expeditious passage of foreign ships and aircraft through Philippine archipelagic waters. It balances the right of foreign passage with the country's national security and environmental protections. Executive Order No. 57 () This is the most recent significant executive issuance related to the region's security. Strengthening Maritime Security: President [REDACTED_NAME] issued this to reorganize and strengthen the country’s maritime security and domain awareness. National Maritime Council: It created the National Maritime Council to centralize the government's response to challenges in the West Philippine Sea, which directly impacts the Kalayaan Islands. Municipality of Kalayaan Local Executive Orders (Municipality of Kalayaan) The Mayor of Kalayaan frequently issues local Executive Orders for municipal governance. EO No. 28, Series of : Created a Technical Working Group to handle the official naming of features within the Kalayaan Island Group. Additionally, in the Philippine government passed laws. Republic Act No. (The Philippine Baselines Law of ): This law defined the archipelagic baselines of the Philippines. It treated the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG) and Bajo de Masinloc (Scarborough Shoal) under a "Regime of Islands," meaning they generate their own maritime zones (like a 12-nautical-mile territorial sea) consistent with UNCLOS. In a conclusion, [REDACTED_NAME] (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) ruling favor on us that we have our rights on our 200 nautical miles EEZ Here is the summary of the Arbitral Ruling of UNCLOS. [REDACTED_NAME] (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea): The Philippines is a signatory to this "Constitution of the Oceans," which provides the legal basis for the 200-nautical-mile EEZ. Arbitral Award: Issued by the Permanent Court of Arbitration in The Hague, this legally binding ruling invalidated China's "nine-dash line" and confirmed that features like [REDACTED_NAME] and Second [REDACTED_NAME] Shoal are within the Philippine [REDACTED_NAME]. erma2 na credentials As someone who looks up to student leaders, I find it disappointing to observe situations where leadership appears to be pursued primarily for personal gain rather than genuine service. Leadership, in my view, should be rooted in sincerity, accountability, and a true commitment to the welfare of others. While credentials are often presented as evidence of qualification, not all listed achievements necessarily reflect meaningful contribution. Some may simply indicate participation rather than active involvement, and others may be overstated or lack substantial impact. For me, real credentials are demonstrated through consistent effort, integrity, and tangible service—not merely through appearances or recognition. This is why claims such as “leading with integrity” should be supported by honest and transparent actions. Any inconsistency between stated values and actual conduct raises valid concerns, especially when leadership positions demand trust and credibility. This statement is not intended as a personal attack, but as an honest and ethical opinion based on observed actions and shared experiences from individuals who have witnessed these matters firsthand. Constructive criticism is necessary in leadership discourse, as it encourages reflection, accountability, and growth for the benefit of the community. Since it’s campaign season, this is really the most anticipated time for student leaders in [REDACTED_NAME]—especially knowing that ANHS has produced so many potential leaders. This time, the aspiring president from the SURE Party somehow looks deserving of the position. HELLO GENTLE READER. Don’t ever stop going to school just because you’re hurt, broken, or drowning in problems. Pain is real, yes but quitting is never the solution. Your education is bigger than the person who hurt you, bigger than the tears you cried at night, bigger than the problems trying to pull you down. Remember this: what you’re going through is temporary, but what you’re building will last a lifetime. The days you feel weak are the exact days you need to show up. Not because it’s easy but because your future self is counting on you. Be strong when everything inside you feels tired. Be brave even when your heart is heavy. Use the pain as fuel, not as an excuse. You don’t study for people who left you, doubted you, or hurt you, you study for your dreams, your family, and the life you want to live. You need to be resilient, disciplined, and focused. Cry if you must, rest if you need but never give up. One day, you’ll look back and say: “I survived that, and I didn’t quit.” And that will be your greatest victory. - Miss [REDACTED_NAME] Entry Date: 2/4/ 0:24:40

"Why did you change?" I have a girlfriend, a cute girl with a happy and playful personality and we've been together for 2 years. Loving a girl with that personality is really great and i couldn't ask for more. But, there's a BUT, when you have a girl like that you know that a gentle soul is a sensitive one. I don't really have a problem with that since my patience HAVE always been long, even though she often gets angry with even the tiniest of things, it's cool with me, i even think it's cute. One time she got angry at me because I can't lend her 15 pesos, just 15 pesos, it's not really a big deal but the problem is that I'M 50 KILOMETERS AWAY and she's got no Gcash and wants me come personally. She got angry at me the whole day and night. When i listen to her stories and chika and i don't reply long replies, she gets angry and says that i never listen to her, unbeknownst her that i memorize every single word she said. It's just that i like listening but have nothing to add. We often fight because of things like that. I don't really mind that, i really don't mind, but what i do mind is when she gets angry the words that i say can't penetrate her mind anymore and she doesn't want to understand whatever i say. A bigger problem is if my words hit a spot where she realizes she's wrong she'll threaten to break up, and not in a jokingly approach but in a really serious manner and completely opposite of her playful nature. She says hurtful things that breaks me and tears me apart, things that you just can't forget and will stab you everytime you recall it. But even after all that, i still talk to her and tell her that I love her, that i know that she's just getting swayed by her anger and i convinced myself again and again that "She doesn't mean it, she's just a girl, don't mind it", then i throw it at the back of my head. THIS HAPPENS OFTEN. I'm writing here just to vent out the things and thoughts that are breaking out from where i stored those memories and moments. Lately i often hear her say that I've changed and I'm not the me that she knew before. That the me now is more irritable and unstable. She blames me for changing but I've never wanted to be like this. I love her and i want her to know that i still do. It's just that i too am just a human. I didn't send this to her coz i know she'll read it and look at the things that she don't like and then be angry like there's no tomorrow HEHE.(a glass half-empty) - Natoy Entry Date: 2/1/ 18:05:13

DEAREST Dear Langlang, Meeting you wasn’t the plan, but it became my favorite plot twist. I can’t imagine that we ended up in this situation without believing that we could still fix it. I respect your decision, but I was so harsh that night. I didn’t think properly, and I didn’t realize how my words would affect you. I still regret letting you go without telling you everything. It’s been five days, and I still can’t move on. I can’t swallow the guilt of what I did, and I can’t sleep without thinking about you every day. I was rude for doing that to you, and I don’t know if you can forgive me. All I know is that you could have, but I pushed you away. You were my first boyfriend, and it’s not easy to let go. I know we only knew each other for a short period of time, which makes it even sadder, because I wanted to know more about you every version of you. But you know what’s been on my mind? The question that keeps echoing is this: what if you only love-bombed me? What if you only loved the idea that I was the one who was there, instead of someone you truly wanted to pursue and that person wasn’t me? Now I’m stuck in a cage I can’t escape from, unable to regain the strength to love someone again. Every version of me chooses you. Every part of my heart chooses you, because I believe we can still fix this. We can still talk in person to fix us. We can still find a reason to stay, and I want to give that chance to both of us. I don’t know if I will ever fall for someone again, because after everything, I still want you. I still want us. For now, I will chase my dreams for my own good. I will build my future. I will strive in college and live by this statement "I will never fall in love again, unless it’s you.” And if time never bends in our favor, at least you will know that I loved you. - akoyalipinmokahithindibatid💔 Entry Date: 1/31/ 20:37:35

Pending Hi guys, aspiring author here! Can I please ask for your honest feedback on my first chapter I've been working on? (For transparency, I used AI to correct spot and address any grammatical errors and inconsistencies) Looks, love, and a good life. All wonderful things to have, but worthless without money. I sighed, meeting the potbellied mayor’s gaze. “[REDACTED_NAME] miss,” he sneered, folding his jeweled fingers. “The payment of forty thousand jellianes is for a party of five. You’re alone, so you get a fourth of the entire amount.” “How preposterous,” I said, stomping my foot. “I am [REDACTED_NAME], the greatest sorceress of the Isles, and I deserve more than this!” “I am being generous,” he said, curling his lips. “Instead of eight thousand jellianes, I will give you ten thousand for your hard work.” I pressed a hand to my face to calm myself, the other gripping my staff. “Payment must match the effort,” he sneered. “You destroyed the goblin army in one blast. That should be enough.” I would have obliterated him if he weren’t a mayor, and if his bloodline didn’t complicate things. His incompetence ruined the estate, yet here he sat, fat and perfumed. “Just give it to me.” With a sigh, he snapped his fingers. A clerk placed a heavy leather bag on the desk. It thudded loudly, the jellianes clinking inside. “Spend it wisely,” the mayor said, smirking. I took the bag and left, my hand still shaking from rage. The mansion was ornate, but outside, the hamlet lay in ruin. Sagging roofs, muddy paths, gaunt townsfolk. Children clutched threadbare cloaks. Old men leaned on broken tools. The air smelled of rot. I sighed. They hadn’t seen a good harvest in years. Goblins had burned the crops, and the mayor had likely pocketed what little remained. Eh, life was like that. Poor people stayed poor if they didn’t claw their way out. I entered the carriage. The horses snorted as we moved forward. The estate faded behind us, replaced by woods and winding roads. Days later, I arrived at the port city of Reylonda. Salt hung in the air. Ships crowded the docks, sails snapping as merchants shouted. Coin moved freely, and opportunity followed it. This city rewarded ambition, or punished it. I wanted to live on my own terms. A fine house. Endless money. No man to obey. No family to feed. Just myself. I spent my evenings in taverns, listening. Rumors were cheaper than ale. “They say the Sky Admiral defected,” one sailor whispered. “Took his ship and crew south, chasing treasures from the old [REDACTED_NAME].” I smirked. [REDACTED_NAME], the kingdom that fell a millennium ago? I'll clear all the ruins in week. “Not dismissed, gone,” another said. “No one knows where he’s gone. But they do know the he'll risk anything just to get what he wants.” I leaned back. So he risks everything. I’ll take the rewards without dying. A drunk merchant whispered, “The island... far to the south. Ships avoid it. Storms gather there. Most who land never return.” Never return, or return broken. Perfect. More gold for me if I go prepared. “Those who return bring nothing but fear,” muttered a captain. “Fear,” I said, almost to myself. Keeps the weak away, leaves the treasure for me. “But they all say one thing,” a boy added, trembling. “There’s treasure. Gold, jewels, things no one has touched.” I grinned. Gold, jewels, power. All mine. Easy. I sipped my drink, my pulse quickening. That island would be simple. With my magic, it would fall before anyone could blink. Maybe this expedition would finally make me rich. Far from that little girl covered in ashes. Far from weakness. Maybe I’ll finally become crazy rich, I whispered to myself, and I felt a thrill at the thought, as if the gold itself was already calling me... whispering, pulling me closer. - Aspiring Author Entry Date: 1/29/ 21:39:50

I just wanted to get this off my chest Hi! You can call me [REDACTED_NAME]. I’m a 4th year student here in [CARAGA], and I have a boy bestfriend and a girl bestfriend. I’ve been in love with my boy bestfriend since high school. He’s two years older than me, and we met because our families are close. We basically grew up together. I always admired him, but it was when I was in Grade 10 and he was in Grade 12 that I realized I had fallen for him. The problem is he only ever saw me as his younger sister. I met my girl bestfriend here in [CARAGA]. From day one, we were inseparable. Same humor, same vibes, same everything. Things started going downhill when they met. I was there when they first shook hands, never knowing I’d unknowingly become the bridge to their love story. They dated for a while, then made it official after a few months. I tried to keep our friendship intact. I swallowed my tears whenever I saw them together or when I became the permanent third wheel on their dates. I hid behind practiced smiles, because they were two people I cherished. I knew confessing or walking away would only hurt all of us so I stayed silent. But the thing is, she can be a bit cruel to him. She’s demanding, especially of his time, even though she knows he’s juggling family, work, their relationship, and grad school. Sometimes I see him sacrifice time with his family just to be with her and that hurts, because I've always known him to be family-oriented. He also loves spoiling her. Even with me, I’ve never seen a bill when I’m with him. But sometimes her demands feel like a little too much like, expensive dates, expensive gifts, even trips. Like when she asked for a weekend getaway to [REDACTED_NAME] for her birthday, knowing he had just bought a second-hand car a month before. She’s never been selfish or insensitive with me, so I don’t know if this is just how she shows affection or if she’s really like that. But it pains me to see the man I love struggling. It hurts to see him give up so much for her demands. Sometimes I catch myself thinking I’d never do that to him if only he loved me. But I never say it out loud. I know it’s wrong. I know I can’t love a man my friend already loves. I don’t know what to do. I’m conflicted. By the way everything I just said was a lie. I just wanted my freedom wall main character moment. I’ve seen so many love stories here so I wanted to try too. Bye! Thanks for reading and sorry for the time you wasted. 😌 - Savie Entry Date: 1/26/ 21:56:20

I love my boyfriend, but his constant fights with his father scare me. How do I talk to him without invalidating his pain? Hi everyone. I’m posting here because I feel really torn and I don’t know what the right thing to do is anymore. I love my boyfriend deeply. We’ve been together for 4 years, and he has always been kind, gentle, and patient with me. He has never shouted at me, never been violent, never made me feel unsafe. That’s the man I know and love. But his relationship with his father is a completely different story. They fight, a lot. Not just arguments, but loud shouting matches that the neighbors can hear. There are moments when my boyfriend ends up saying really hurtful things to his dad. But please don’t judge him yet. My boyfriend passed his board exam about three years ago, but he’s still unemployed. He does side jobs to cover his needs while continuously applying for work. Where we live, having connections matters a lot (backers ba), and it hasn’t been easy for him. His father constantly brings up how he “wasted” his education and compares him to other people. What hurts me is that I’ve seen the other side, the side most people don’t see. I witnessed how hard my boyfriend struggled just to finish school. He practically raised himself. His father never supported him financially, and emotionally, since childhood. He told me there were times in high school when he didn’t even have shoes and had to find ways to buy things for himself. He worked side jobs while studying college because even though he was a scholar, the scholarship wasn’t enough. He’s genuinely smart. He took his board exam through self-review alone in two weeks, and missed being a topnotcher by just a few points (imagine that). The painful part is that he didn’t even want his degree, he only took it because it was the only program his scholarship covered. So when his father calls him things like “a burden,” something inside him just snaps. He answers back, saying his father was never really a father to him. Sometimes the words get even worse, and every time I hear it, my chest hurts. I only visit their house around four times a year, pero every single time I'm there, they fight (according [REDACTED_NAME] him, almost every day [REDACTED_NAME] away tapos mas worst pa). I always end up wanting to cry pagmakadungog [REDACTED_NAME] call lang. I grew up in a healthy home man gud, so hearing their arguments feels overwhelming and painful. His father is already old, and I feel ashamed, sad, and helpless all at once. Now I’m stuck. Part of me wants to tell my boyfriend, “He’s still your father, you should respect him.” But another part of me knows that saying that might invalidate years of pain, neglect, and resentment he’s been carrying alone. I’m also scared of the future. What if one day we have our own family and this unresolved anger shows up again? What about our future children? I don’t want them growing up around shouting and emotional wounds that never healed. I love him so much, but I don’t know how to bring this up without hurting him or making him feel like I’m choosing his father’s side. How do I talk to him about this gently? How do I express my fears without invalidating his pain? Or is this something I should stay out of completely? Any advice would mean a lot. Thank you for reading. - Concerned And Worried Girlfriend Entry Date: 1/26/ 20:15:11

Not Everything Is a Joke It’s , and it’s concerning that some people still think comments about skin color or appearance are harmless jokes. They are not. Calling something a joke does not automatically make it acceptable. It’s disappointing that this kind of behavior still happens in college spaces, where awareness and maturity should already be present. Remarks about someone’s color or looks are not casual banter. They are insensitive, disrespectful, and unnecessary. This post is for awareness. Joking about someone’s appearance or skin color can have a deeper impact than you realize. You do not get to decide how someone else feels simply because you intended it “as a joke.” Growth shows in knowing when to stop. Decency shows in choosing not to say everything that crosses your mind. - Tsk Entry Date: 1/25/ 20:30:14

Just want to share I’m currently a freshman at [CARAGA], and I just want to rant because I’m bored—and honestly, because I need advice. When I was younger, I was very active. I played outside a lot, both around our house and at school. I had many friends—friends I played with, talked to, and shared a normal childhood with. Life felt simple back then. Then the pandemic started—[REDACTED_NAME]. I was in Grade 7, about to enter Grade 8. At first, I thought everything would be fine. I told myself I could still talk to my friends and play with them online. But as time passed, we slowly drifted apart. I became addicted to online games, stopped focusing on my academics, and I started to lose myself. I wasn’t improving—I was glowing down. Fast forward to Grade 10. The pandemic was almost over, and face-to-face classes returned. I was classmates with some of my old friends again. There was also this beautiful girl from another section. We had been classmates before, and she often came to our room just to hang out with me or check on me. At the time, I didn’t think much of it. I wasn’t planning to date anyone, and I had heard she might already have an MU, so I didn’t make any moves. Still, I admired her from afar. What I didn’t know was that she liked me too. Then there was another girl in our section—also beautiful. I didn’t think anything would happen between us. One day, her things were stolen by a classmate, and I helped comfort her because I’ve always liked helping people. After that, she started hanging out with me more. At first, I saw her as just a classmate, and I didn’t like her that way. But as days passed and we spent more time together, I started to develop feelings—mostly because I felt that she liked me too. Eventually, we got into a relationship. It was the wrong timing. She had just broken up with her ex-MU, and I didn’t know she had been with him for four years. We started dating just a week after their breakup. Our relationship was messy. We fought over small things—mostly because I was immature and didn’t know how to be a good boyfriend. We would break up and get back together the next day, or even the same day. The fights kept repeating until one day, I broke up with her for real. I was overwhelmed and angry, and I said things I didn’t mean. The next day, I asked her if we could talk, and she agreed. I asked if we could get back together. She said no. I asked her, “Are we really breaking up? Is this it?” She said yes again. I begged her to come back—not because it was right, but because I was selfish. Since the pandemic, she was the only person I had. She was my everything—my first love—and losing her broke me. She told me that maybe we could try dating again after college. I agreed. I asked her, “What if you find another man while I wait?” She told me it wouldn’t happen. A week later, she came to our classroom and asked if we could be together again because she couldn’t stand being alone. I didn’t say yes immediately—I thought maybe we both needed time. She left without saying anything else. That night, she texted me and told me not to mind what she said earlier. I replied that it was okay. A week later, I found out she already had someone new. I was shocked—but not completely. Deep down, I expected it to happen, just not that fast. I confronted her. She had nothing to say. I asked her, “What about our plan to try again after college?” She didn’t answer. I was angry and hurt, so I blocked her and never spoke to her again. Now I’m in college. I have no friends, no motivation, and honestly, no will to live sometimes. I feel empty—just a lonely person holding onto a small hope that my life can still change. Right now, I’m thinking about the first girl—the one who liked me before everything went wrong. I want to talk to her again and see her, but I want to become a better version of myself first. So... what should I do? - — Mr. [REDACTED_NAME] Entry Date: 1/24/ 23:39:03

He only talked to me at night. I didn’t question it then. I thought that was just how his schedule worked. Later on, I understood why it was always at night. He wasn’t single. He had a long-term GF—someone he was fully committed to. Someone he plans to marry in the future. While she was trusting him, building a future with him, he was talking to me in secret. Many people have prayed to be in this school, and not everyone who desires it is given the chance to study here. That’s why I’m truly thankful that I’m here right now. Honestly, everything about OBE is only now starting to sink in for me. Last semester, it was mostly present in our minor subjects, but now even our major subjects are OBE-based. I can’t stop thinking about the what ifs. What if I don’t make it? What if I don’t finish? Little by little, fear starts to replace my optimistic side, and sometimes it feels heavy to carry alone. Still, I choose to believe that this season will shape me, not break me. Hello guys. There’s this girl my boyfriend liked before, even though we were still together—or at least I thought we were. The girl didn’t feel the same way about him, but she did give him attention. It’s not her fault, because she didn’t know he already had a girlfriend. Only a few of his classmates knew about me, and I guess they assumed we had broken up since he was very obvious and vocal to his friends about liking her. After three months of no contact, he texted me again. During that time, I was forcing myself to move on from him—but I couldn’t. I realized I still loved him despite everything he did and despite all the pain I went through. We eventually got back together, but I still can’t stop myself from stalking the girl’s social media. He told me that he already forgot about her and that I should do the same. But my heart and mind won’t cooperate. A part of me wants to forget everything and move on, but it still hurts so much. Last night, I dreamed that he liked her again and left me—choosing her over me once more. I woke up crying. I just really can’t forget what happened. I only hope he realizes that my fear now comes from broken trust, not jealousy. During that time, I was even hiding myself from his social media—acting like I didn’t exist—just so she wouldn’t know that he already had me, especially because I noticed she reacted to his posts. To the Vice President for Academic Affairs, With due respect to your office, this letter is written to formally express our deep disappointment regarding the recent disapproval of our academic request, justified solely on the basis that the requesting students are not yet graduating. While it is true that the subject being requested is intended for lower-year students, this does not diminish its importance. On the contrary, the course is a crucial prerequisite for several higher-level subjects that the students are required to take in the succeeding terms. As a result, a simple disapproval has effectively led to the extension of the students’ stay in the university by at least one additional year. This consequence is not minor, nor is it easily reversible. What adds to the frustration is that the students had already taken concrete and responsible steps to address the situation. A qualified instructor had agreed to handle the course, and a feasible schedule had been carefully arranged. The students fully acknowledge their own lapses that contributed to this circumstance, and they are making sincere efforts to correct them. These actions reflect accountability and commitment, values that we believe an academic institution should nurture. To be denied solely because the majority of the requesting students are not graduating, despite the academic necessity of the course, is both discouraging and unsettling. It gives the impression that classification outweighs context, and that genuine attempts to move forward academically can be set aside without regard for their long-term impact. It is difficult to hide a sense of restrained anger when a decision made so simply carries such serious consequences for students’ academic and personal lives. Still, this letter is written with hope. We earnestly hope that future requests will not be evaluated solely on whether the majority of requesting students are graduating or not. Instead, we hope that equal weight will be given to the nature of the course being requested and how essential it is to the students’ program of study. A more holistic and considerate approach to such decisions would go a long way in preventing unnecessary delays and in reaffirming students’ trust in academic leadership. - Emotions Unwinding Entry Date: 1/21/ 22:03:37

Selfish I will no longer sugarcoat my words. This is me choosing honesty. Communication Commitment You lack both. You hide behind avoidance, using it to deflect confrontation. You retreat into it when things get real. Is it funny to you, stringing me along? You said you were ready for this, ready for our relationship. So why does it feel like the moment I put in effort, you begin to pull away? Were your words just bait, meant to keep me orbiting you? Those late-night calls, the laughter, the shared moments were they all a facade? Yes, you opened up. But slowly, quietly, you closed that door the moment I tried to do the same. Do you truly love me, or only love me when it’s convenient? Don’t get me wrong I have my own flaws. But even the stars know how hard I’m trying. Trying to change, to heal, to believe in love again. And it’s starting to feel like all of this is in vain. I hate that I feel this way. I hate that I doubt you. I hate that my thoughts go there. I’ve given you time. I’ve given you chances. I’ve given you the benefit of the doubt. Because I love you. Because I believe in you. Because I placed my faith in you. But do you believe in yourself? Do you believe in us? You said you wanted this. You said you’d try. Yet the moment things get difficult, you disappear into silence. No communication. No reassurance. Just absence leaving me walking on eggshells, unsure of how you are, unsure of how you see us. How do you expect things to change if you do nothing to change them? You know it hurts. You know the pain you’ve caused. You entered this relationship aware of the commitment it required, yet you refuse to meet it. It’s draining us. It’s hurting us both. And still, you choose the comfort of silence over the discomfort of growth. You choose stillness over something that could heal us. And maybe that’s selfish. And yet from the bottom of my heart I still love you. I embrace all your flaws, even your avoidance, with open arms. I choose to wait because I still believe in you, and I still believe in us. I’m waiting for the day you realize what we have is real. Waiting for the moment you understand that love requires change. And maybe this- this waiting is me being selfish too. - 🐞 Entry Date: 1/21/ 8:25:52

Concern Regarding TES Cash Release Prioritization We were encouraged by the TES staff to open ATM savings accounts to make transactions easier and more efficient. Some students followed this instruction, complied early, and completed all the required paperwork. Others, however, chose not to open bank accounts and were told they would instead receive cash on hand. Now that the cash release day has arrived, it is frustrating to see that students who did not comply with the advised process are being prioritized, while those who were obedient and responsible are left waiting with no clear updates on when our funds will be released. There has been no timeline, no announcement, and no explanation for the delay. What makes this more confusing is that releasing funds through bank accounts should be easier, faster, and more organized. Yet, those who followed the instructions are the ones experiencing uncertainty and inconvenience. This situation feels unfair and discouraging. It sends the wrong message—that compliance and cooperation are not valued. We only ask for transparency, proper communication, and fair treatment for all TES grantees, especially those who followed the given instructions. This message is not meant to attack anyone, but to voice a genuine concern that many students are silently feeling. Respectfully, A concerned TES grantee - TES grantee Entry Date: 1/20/ 21:32:49
